description: Batch sequence analysis via gget: BLAST each record in a FASTA file (gget blast) then run a MUSCLE multiple alignment over all records (gget muscle). Writes per-sequence BLAST CSVs and an alignment.afa under record_store (read-only remote queries).
rules: read-only — only queries the NCBI BLAST + MUSCLE services and writes local files
needs network and an installed gget package; with gget absent the porter degrades gracefully and still writes a non-empty report
all outputs land under record_store (the porter runs the vendored core there)
AlphaFold structure prediction in the core is disabled by default (the porter never passes --predict-structure)
usage: Set FASTA (path to a FASTA file of sequences) and BLAST_DB (default nr for proteins, nt for nucleotides). Output: sequence_analysis.log (run log) plus <id>_blast.csv per record and alignment.afa when >1 sequence.
limitation: Requires gget + network for real BLAST/alignment results; offline it only produces the run log. Alignment is skipped for a single sequence. Per-sequence BLAST failures are warned and skipped.
